questions: Should parameter changes in the ClipPanel update the clip in real-time (optimistic, immediate re-render) or require a 'apply' button? The current pattern for other clip properties (position, opacity, volume) is immediate — I'll follow that unless you want a debounced or deferred approach for params.

For the LLM output format: I'm proposing a simple `// PARAMS: [...]` comment-based convention rather than asking the LLM to return structured JSON (which would require changing from plain-text code output to a JSON envelope). An alternative is to have the LLM return a JSON object `{ code: '...', parameterSchema: [...] }` — but this is riskier with code-focused models. Preference?

Should built-in effects also get tuneable parameters eventually, or is this strictly for custom/LLM-generated effects? This affects whether ParameterControls should render for built-in effects too (the type system will support it either way).

When an effect's schema changes (e.g., user re-generates with different params), should existing clips using that effect reset their param values to new defaults, keep stale values (ignoring removed params), or merge (keep valid, reset removed/new)?
success_criteria: TypeScript compiles cleanly (`tsc --noEmit`) with all new types and extended interfaces
LLM-generated effects include a `parameterSchema` array in the edge function response
ParameterControls renders the correct control type (slider/select/switch/color) for each parameter definition
Creator panel preview updates in real-time when tuneable parameter values change
Parameter schema is persisted in the effect resource's JSONB metadata
Per-clip parameter values are stored in `clip.entrance.params` / `clip.exit.params` / `clip.continuous.params`
ClipPanel shows parameter controls when a custom effect with schema is selected on a clip
Effect component receives `params` prop at render time and the rendered output reflects param values
Existing effects (built-in and custom without params) continue to work unchanged
Parameter values persist across page reload (stored in timeline config JSON)
assumptions: Immediate/optimistic updates for param changes in ClipPanel (matching existing clip property patterns like opacity, volume)
The `// PARAMS: [...]` comment convention for LLM output is safer than a JSON envelope for code-generation models — using this unless told otherwise
Empty `parameterSchema` (or absent field) means no tuneable params — fully backward compatible with existing effects
No database migration needed: `parameterSchema` goes in existing JSONB `metadata` column on `resources` table; `params` goes in existing clip JSON fields
Built-in effects will NOT get tuneable parameters in this iteration — controls only render for custom effects with a schema
When an effect's schema changes, existing clip param values are merged: valid values kept, new params get defaults, removed params are silently ignored (the component won't read them)
The Groq/Kimi K2 model can reliably generate the `// PARAMS:` annotation — will add a fallback that defaults to empty schema if parsing fails
